---
title: Audit & History
description: Build product-facing history views and long-lived audit trails with Syncular
---

# Audit & History

Use this guide for product-facing history views: recent sync history, conflict context, and long-lived app-owned audit trails.

If you are investigating incidents or operating a live deployment, use the Console guides in [Operate](/docs/operate/console) instead.

## Use this page when

- you want users or admins inside your product to inspect recent sync history
- you need a durable business or compliance audit trail that outlives sync retention
- you want a clear boundary between product-facing history and operator-only forensics

## What you'll build

- product-visible commit history
- conflict-aware history UI
- app-owned row audit records for long retention
- links from product history into deeper operator workflows when needed

## Prerequisites

- a working Syncular app with authenticated actors
- familiarity with [Commits](/docs/concepts/commits) and [Scopes](/docs/concepts/scopes)

## Choose the right history surface

| Need | Use |
|---|---|
| Product-facing recent sync history for authorized users | This guide plus `GET /sync/audit/commits` |
| Long-lived business or compliance audit records | Your own `audit_log` table |
| Incident forensics, request payloads, and client lag investigation | [Console Stream Investigation](/docs/operate/console/stream-investigation) |
| Maintenance action history such as prune, compact, or notify-data-change | [Console Operations](/docs/operate/console/operations) |

## Recommended building blocks

1. Client push outcomes: `push:result` / `onPushResult`
2. Conflict stream for UX: `useNewConflicts()` + `useConflicts()`
3. Commit attribution in apply handlers: `commitSeq`, `actorId`, `createdAt`
4. Scoped API history: `/sync/audit/commits` and `/sync/audit/commits/{commitSeq}`

## Client UX Patterns

### Immediate push feedback

```tsx
<SyncProvider
  db={db}
  transport={transport}
  sync={sync}
  identity={{ actorId: userId }}
  onPushResult={(result) => {
    if (result.status === 'rejected') {
      showToast(`Commit ${result.clientCommitId} rejected (${result.errorCode ?? 'unknown'})`);
    }
  }}
>
  <App />
</SyncProvider>
```

### New conflict notifications

```tsx
const { useNewConflicts } = createSyncularReact<AppDb>();

function ConflictNotifications() {
  const { latest } = useNewConflicts();
  useEffect(() => {
    if (!latest) return;
    showToast(`Conflict on ${latest.table}:${latest.rowId}`);
  }, [latest]);
  return null;
}
```

## Sync Audit API

Use auth-scoped endpoints:

- `GET /sync/audit/commits`
- `GET /sync/audit/commits/{commitSeq}`

These endpoints are partition/auth scoped and intended for product admin UI, not console-only operator workflows.

## App-Owned Row Audit Recipe

Sync history is optimized for sync and may be pruned or compacted. If you need long-lived row-level audit records, keep an app-owned `audit_log` table.

Suggested schema:

```sql
create table audit_log (
  id bigserial primary key,
  table_name text not null,
  row_id text not null,
  op text not null, -- insert | update | delete
  actor_id text,
  commit_seq bigint,
  changed_at timestamptz not null default now(),
  before_hash text,
  after_hash text,
  before_snapshot jsonb,
  after_snapshot jsonb
);

create index audit_log_table_row_changed_at_idx
  on audit_log (table_name, row_id, changed_at desc);

create index audit_log_actor_changed_at_idx
  on audit_log (actor_id, changed_at desc);
```
